meta.title: Bread Baking in Calgary | Mixler
meta.description: Learn to bake real bread in Calgary. Mix, knead, shape, and bake a loaf from scratch at a hands-on class. Take your fresh-baked bread home the same day.

content.subtitle: Make a real loaf of bread by hand and take it home warm from the oven.
content.intro: Bread baking classes in Calgary are one of the most grounding creative food experiences you can have. There's something deeply satisfying about working dough with your hands and pulling a finished loaf out of the oven. A baker guides you through every step, from mixing and kneading to shaping and scoring, and you leave with bread you actually baked.

what_to_expect.heading: Understanding flour and hydration
what_to_expect.body: You'll learn how different flours behave and why the water-to-flour ratio changes the texture of your loaf.

what_to_expect.heading: Kneading and gluten development
what_to_expect.body: The instructor shows you what properly developed dough feels like. You knead until you get there.

what_to_expect.heading: Shaping and scoring
what_to_expect.body: Shaping gives the loaf structure. Scoring controls where it opens in the oven. Both are techniques with real technique behind them.

what_to_expect.heading: Take your bread home
what_to_expect.body: Your loaf goes in the oven and comes out before the session ends. You take it home warm.

tips: Don't rush the fermentation. Longer, cooler rises develop more flavour. An overnight fridge rise is worth the wait.
A Dutch oven produces the best home oven crust. The steam from the covered pot mimics a professional bread oven.
Calgary's altitude (just over 1000m) can affect rise times slightly. Your instructor will account for this.
Baking bread at home is genuinely addictive once you start. The class will likely be the beginning of a habit.
Mixler lists bread baking events in Calgary when they're on. Join the waitlist and we'll email you when one is added.
content.waitlist_heading: Want to know when we run bread baking events?
content.waitlist_body: Join the waitlist and we'll email you when we add one. We use this to plan what to run next.

faq.a: No. Classes are designed for beginners. You'll learn everything from scratch, including how to tell when your dough is ready.

faq.q: What type of bread will I make?
faq.a: It depends on the class. Common options include sourdough, focaccia, or a simple white loaf. The listing will specify.

faq.q: Do I take my bread home?
faq.a: Yes. Your loaf comes out of the oven during the session. You go home with fresh-baked bread.

faq.q: How long does a bread baking class take?
faq.a: Most sessions run 3 hours or more because bread takes time. The waiting is a natural part of the experience.
